status_definitions.approved: License verified as public domain or an open license (e.g. CC BY-SA). Safe to collect and redistribute with attribution where required.
status_definitions.conditional: Officially free to download from the rights holder, but NOT openly licensed. Personal, non-commercial educational use only; no redistribution of content. Collection requires explicit project-owner sign-off.
status_definitions.rejected: Terms prohibit collection, provenance is unverifiable, or the material is under copyright without permission.

sources.name: Scholastic Aptitude Test (1926) - the original first SAT

evidence.note: Wikimedia Commons file page carries a US public-domain tag stating the work was published (or registered with the US Copyright Office) before January 1, 1931.

sources.decision_reason: US public domain (pre-1931 publication). No usage restrictions.
sources.notes: Historic first SAT (administered June 23, 1926). Nine subtests including analogies, antonyms, number series, paragraph reading.

sources.name: Examination Questions in Mathematics, Third Series (1911-1915)
sources.publisher: College Entrance Examination Board / Ginn and Company
sources.file_resolution: Resolve concrete PDF filename via https://archive.org/metadata/questmathematics00collrich in Phase 2
sources.license: Public domain (US): published before 1931; Internet Archive rights statement NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT

evidence.note: Internet Archive item page; CEEB mathematics examination questions, published by Ginn and Company. Free download offered (no lending restriction), consistent with not-in-copyright status.

sources.decision_reason: US public domain (pre-1931 publication); Internet Archive offers unrestricted download.
sources.notes: Closest historical analogue to SAT math sections. Good volume of math questions for pipeline testing.

sources.name: Examination Questions in Mathematics, Fourth Series (1916-1920)
sources.publisher: College Entrance Examination Board / Ginn and Company
sources.file_resolution: Resolve concrete PDF filename via https://archive.org/metadata/mathematicsquest00collrich in Phase 2

evidence.note: Internet Archive item page; CEEB mathematics examination questions 1916-1920, free download offered.

sources.decision_reason: US public domain (pre-1931 publication); unrestricted download.
sources.notes: Companion volume to the third series.

sources.name: Examination Questions in History (c. 1915)
sources.publisher: College Entrance Examination Board / Ginn and Company
sources.file_resolution: Resolve concrete PDF filename via https://archive.org/metadata/questexamination00collrich in Phase 2
sources.license: Public domain (US): Internet Archive rights statement NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT

evidence.note: Internet Archive metadata explicitly shows NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT; full free PDF download offered (B/W PDF 1.5M, standard PDF 2.0M, full text, EPUB). 47 pages, scanned from University of California Libraries.

sources.decision_reason: Explicit NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT rights statement plus pre-1931 publication.
sources.notes: Verbal/essay-style questions; useful for testing text-heavy page routing.

sources.name: Examination Questions in History, 1901-1905
sources.file_resolution: Resolve concrete PDF filename via https://archive.org/metadata/examinationques01boargoog in Phase 2
sources.license: Public domain (US): published before 1931 (Google Books scan)

evidence.note: Internet Archive item (Google Books scan) of CEEB history examination questions 1901-1905; publication predates 1931 by 26+ years.

sources.notes: Oldest item in the registry; also useful as an OCR stress test (older typography).

collection.reason: HTML wiki with almost no question content; approved on license but deprioritized for collection

sources.name: Wikibooks: SAT Study Guide

evidence.note: Page footer states text is available under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike License. However the book is ~0% developed: practice-test section is an empty stub; only a few review subsections have content.

sources.decision_reason: Valid open license (CC BY-SA), but content is nearly empty - no actual practice questions. Kept in registry for completeness; deprioritized for collection.
sources.notes: HTML source, not PDF. Would need a different collection path; low expected yield.

collection.owner_signoff: Granted by project owner on 2026-07-15: internal educational use only, outputs must stay private, no redistribution.
collection.automation_note: College Board's CDN resets connections from non-browser clients (verified 2026-07-15), and PDF links are rendered client-side. Collection therefore goes through the manual-intake path: download in a normal browser, drop into data/raw/_inbox/collegeboard-paper-practice-tests/, run downloader --intake.

sources.name: College Board Official SAT Paper Practice Tests (PDF)
sources.file_resolution: Individual practice-test PDF links to be enumerated from landing page if collection is authorized
sources.license: Copyright College Board, all rights reserved. Free download; personal, non-commercial educational use only; redistribution and online re-posting prohibited without written permission.

evidence.note: Official practice-tests page confirms free full-length paper (nonadaptive) practice test PDFs are offered for download.

SAT Suite Program Agreement: College Board is the exclusive owner of all rights in the SAT exam and all individual test items; posting/reproducing questions online without express written permission is prohibited. Official practice tests may be used by students in noncommercial educational settings.
College Board maintains a formal copyright permission request process, confirming materials are not openly licensed.

sources.decision_reason: Free and official, but NOT openly licensed. Downloading for personal educational use is consistent with College Board terms; redistribution of the PDFs or extracted questions is not. Requires explicit project-owner sign-off before collection, and outputs must stay private.
sources.notes: Direct fetches of collegeboard.org terms pages were blocked (connection reset / timeout - likely bot protection); license position verified via College Board's own program-agreement and copyright-permission pages found through search. Re-verify manually in a browser before any collection.

sources.name: College Board SAT Suite Educator Question Bank
sources.license: Copyright College Board. Terms explicitly prohibit uploading, posting, caching, reproducing, or modifying any portion of the SAT Suite Question Bank without express written permission.

evidence.note: Program agreement prohibits caching/reproducing/scraping the SAT Suite Question Bank without express written permission from College Board.

sources.decision_reason: Terms explicitly prohibit exactly what this pipeline would do (caching/reproducing question bank items). Official and free does not mean collectible.
sources.notes: 3,500+ questions for educators, but scraping is expressly forbidden.

sources.name: Khan Academy Official SAT Practice
sources.publisher: Khan Academy (content owned by College Board)
sources.license: Khan Academy ToS: personal non-commercial use only; scraping/automated extraction prohibited; AI/ML training use prohibited. SAT items remain College Board copyright.

evidence.note: Khan Academy help center: ToS prohibits scraping tools/bots to copy or extract data, and prohibits use of content to train/test/develop AI or ML tools.
Khan Academy Terms of Service (personal, non-commercial use).

sources.decision_reason: ToS explicitly prohibits scraping and automated extraction; underlying SAT content is College Board copyright.

sources.name: CrackSAT.net practice test collection
sources.license: No valid license. Site redistributes College Board copyrighted practice tests without evident authorization.

evidence.note: Site's own copyright page claims content copyright by a private individual, with no evidence of a College Board license or permission.
College Board requires written permission for reproduction of its test materials; no indication CrackSAT holds such permission.

sources.decision_reason: Unauthorized redistribution of copyrighted College Board materials. Collecting from it would launder infringing copies into the pipeline.
sources.notes: Classic example of a free-but-not-legal source.

sources.name: OpenSAT (GitHub question databank + API)
sources.license: Repository code is open source, but the license of the question CONTENT is unclear and provenance is unverifiable (community-submitted and AI-generated items; risk of derivation from College Board copyrighted questions).

evidence.note: Open-source repo offering a free SAT question databank and JSON API; README describes community contributions and an AI question generator. No documented provenance or content license separating it from College Board items.

sources.decision_reason: A code license does not license the question content. Provenance of items is unverifiable and may derive from copyrighted College Board material; AI-generated items also lack quality assurance.
sources.notes: Could be revisited if the project publishes a clear content license and provenance audit.

sources.name: 10 SATs: actual and complete Scholastic Aptitude Tests (1983+ book scans)
sources.license: In copyright. Internet Archive access is controlled digital lending only (borrow), not free download.

evidence.note: Archive.org item is a 1983 College Board publication available via lending, not open download; publication postdates 1930 so no public-domain basis.

sources.decision_reason: Under copyright (published 1983); lending access does not grant collection or reproduction rights.

sources.name: Real SAT Subject Tests (book scan)
sources.license: In copyright. Controlled digital lending only.

evidence.note: Modern College Board publication on archive.org via lending; no public-domain or open-license basis.

sources.decision_reason: Under copyright; lending access does not grant collection rights.
